notes: No-yield variant still functional after Token-2022 changes. 14/14 actions, 0 crashes, 23.9% ok ratio. No regression.

Prewritten ExtGlobalV2 used the serialized None-length instead of max-size allocation, so transfer_admin->Some(new_admin) overflowed the parser path. Fixed by enlarging the prewritten account and removing dynamic global parsing from the execution path.
Dry-run passed with 15 tracked accounts and 1 loaded program.
Coverage reached 168/8142 edges and 153/4071 branches with actions/exec 2.5, but ok remained 0/326. The harness now mutates state without crashing, yet positive instruction success is still missing.
Dry-run passed with 18 tracked accounts and 1 loaded program.
Coverage reached 168/9650 edges and 153/4825 branches with actions/exec 4.6, but ok remained 0/1787. Stateful action mixing is live, yet account ordering or state realism still blocks successful txs.

Coverage reached 168/9326 edges and 153/4663 branches with actions/exec 4.1, but ok remained 0/3649. Variant switching works; successful scaled-ui instruction execution still needs better account realism.

binary_or_command: KAST_VARIANT=no-yield KAST_TRACE_ERRORS=1 crucible run kast invariant_test --release --timeout 2 --cores 1
notes: Tracing showed every attempted instruction failed with Anchor DeclaredProgramIdMismatch. Root cause was the harness hard-coding the wrong byte array for PROGRAM_ID while the comment string was correct.
Positive paths unlocked. Coverage reached 630/8142 edges and 561/4071 branches with actions/exec 6.3 and ok 17805/40417, without crashes.
Positive crank paths now execute. Coverage reached 1163/9650 edges and 1070/4825 branches with actions/exec 6.4 and ok 12713/25354, without crashes.
Positive scaled-ui paths now execute. Coverage reached 637/9326 edges and 568/4663 branches with actions/exec 4.9 and ok 19911/37902, without crashes.

Longer crank follow-up stayed crash-free and improved throughput. Coverage held at 1163/9650 edges and 1070/4825 branches while ok rose to 31137/55712 with actions/exec 6.6. No measured-impact signal emerged from the currently modeled lifecycle paths.

Longer scaled-ui follow-up stayed crash-free and raised ok to 45444/80331 with actions/exec 5.9. Coverage remained 637/9326 edges and 568/4663 branches; the current harness still does not exercise Token-2022 sync/claim-fee rebasing paths directly.

binary_or_command: PYTHONPATH=src python3 inline search over compute_sync_outcome / compute_crank_claim_outcome
notes: Lightweight offline search found no stepwise-vs-direct sync drift in the modeled integer-index transitions and confirmed fee idempotency for repeated identical claim inputs. It did surface a conditional freeze state when global_index regresses below last_claim_index, but on-chain reachability is unproven and depends on non-monotonic M-side index behavior.

notes: Corrected Token-2022 TLV offsets: AccountType at 165 (not 82), TLV data at 170+. Fee/user/vault token accounts extended to 170 bytes with proper AccountType. 17/17 actions discovered. 1537 edges/16.5%, 1397 branches/30.0%, ok=13547/24133 (56.1%). 0 crashes. No InvalidAccountData errors.

binary_or_command: KAST_VARIANT=crank KAST_TRACE_ERRORS=1 timeout 65 cargo run --bin invariant_test --features invariant_test
notes: Crank after TLV fix: 1796 edges/18.6%, 1674 branches/34.7%, ok=10585/20044 (52.8%). 17/17 actions discovered. Full multisync_claim chain verified (update_multiplier→sync→claim_for). AlreadyClaimed actively firing. No InvalidAccountData. 0 crashes. Freeze analysis: AlreadyClaimed is intentional, non-griefable anti-replay. No abuse path found.

Discovered incorrect collateral check in claim_for.rs:137. Uses INDEX_SCALE instead of ext_index for EXT value scaling. When ext_index > INDEX_SCALE, allows over-minting EXT tokens beyond vault capacity. Python test confirms ~5% over-mint per claim at ext_index=1.1e12. Added action_claim_for_big_snapshot and action_claim_for_inflated fuzzer actions to demonstrate the bug. Fuzzer now has 19 actions.

H5 finding RECANTED. The claim_for collateral check at rs:137 correctly compares EXT supply+rewards (in current M value) against vault current value. In crank mode, EXT tokens do NOT have ScaledUiAmount. The (ext_supply + rewards) and ext_collateral = principal_to_amount_down(vault.amount, m_index) are both in current M value units. Python test and adjudication file removed. Initial analysis confused crank mode (plain EXT, no multiplier) with scaled-ui mode (EXT has ScaledUiAmountConfig).
